title: Sweet Sesame Portuguese Stew
description: A rich and indulgent Portuguese stew featuring sesame butter and spherified ingredients, made in a traditional cataplana vessel, this dish is perfect for special occasions and can be adapted to be sugar free friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the cataplana vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chicken breast and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the sesame butter and chicken broth, then pour the mixture over the ingredients in the cataplana.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20 minutes.
While the stew is cooking, prepare the spherification bath by mixing the sodium alginate and water in a small bowl.
Once the stew has finished cooking, remove the chicken and vegetables from the cataplana and set aside.
Drop small spoonfuls of the sesame butter mixture into the spherification bath, creating small, bouncy balls.
Serve the stew hot, topped with the spherified sesame butter balls.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
